name: gh-ticket
description: Create or update comprehensive GitHub issues that capture ALL context, requirements, and implementation details. Use when creating or updating tickets, issues, feature requests, or bug reports. Ensures no context is lost between ticket creation and implementation - the moment you ask for a ticket, there's maximum context available that will be lost if not captured now.
capabilities: Capture context at the moment of discovery before details are forgotten
Structure tickets with requirements, edge cases, and acceptance criteria
Cite exact file paths and line numbers for implementation guidance
Generate Mermaid diagrams to visualize complex workflows
Update existing issues with new context found during investigation
Create standardized ticket formats with consistent sections
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill consisting of a single SKILL.md file (721 lines). The static analyzer flagged 171 patterns in code examples within documentation, but all are false positives. The skill contains NO executable code, no file system access, no network calls, no command execution, and no environment variable access. It only provides markdown templates and guidance for writing comprehensive GitHub issues.
valueStatement: Context is lost the moment you stop thinking about a problem. This skill captures maximum context at ticket creation time, including requirements, implementation details, file locations, and acceptance criteria. The result is tickets that developers can implement immediately without asking follow-up questions.
userTitle: Create comprehensive GitHub issues with full context
limitations: Does not execute git commands or create issues directly (provides templates for manual use)
Requires user to gather initial context before using the skill
Does not integrate with project management tools beyond GitHub

useCases.title: Document bugs with full context
useCases.description: Create bug reports that include error messages, file locations, and steps to reproduce

useCases.title: Define feature requirements
useCases.description: Write feature requests with functional requirements, edge cases, and clear acceptance criteria

useCases.title: Improve ticket quality
useCases.description: Transform vague requests into comprehensive tickets that developers can start immediately

promptTemplates.title: Bug report
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub issue for a bug. The error occurred when [describe action]. The error message was: [paste error]. Files involved: [list files]. Current behavior: [what happens]. Expected behavior: [what should happen].

promptTemplates.title: Feature request
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a GitHub issue for a feature request. Background: [why this is needed]. Current state: [what exists now]. Desired state: [what should exist]. List 5 specific functional requirements as checkboxes.

promptTemplates.title: Update issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Update GitHub issue #123 with new context discovered during investigation. Found additional requirements: [list]. Related files: [list]. Error traces: [paste].

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive spec
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive GitHub issue using the full template. Context: [what triggered this]. Files examined: [list]. Findings: [what was discovered]. Requirements: [detailed list]. Implementation notes: [file paths, line numbers, suggestions]. Acceptance criteria: [testable outcomes].

bestPractices: Capture context immediately when you discover an issue - details fade fast
Cite exact file paths and line numbers so developers do not need to search
Break requirements into individual checkboxes with specific, testable outcomes
antiPatterns: Writing vague requirements like 'fix the auth bug' instead of specific behaviors
Missing error messages or stack traces that help developers diagnose issues
Creating tickets without acceptance criteria, leaving done state undefined

faq.question: Does this skill create GitHub issues directly?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides templates and guidance for writing comprehensive issues. You then use gh CLI or GitHub web interface.

faq.question: What makes a ticket comprehensive?
faq.answer: A comprehensive ticket includes background context, current vs desired state, specific requirements as checkboxes, edge cases, and file locations.

faq.question: Can I use this with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates work for any GitHub repository. Ensure you have appropriate permissions to create or edit issues.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-based skill with no code execution. It only generates text templates. No data is sent to external services.

faq.question: How is this different from GitHub issue templates?
faq.answer: GitHub issue templates are static forms. This skill provides dynamic guidance that adapts to your specific context.

faq.question: Should I create a new ticket or update an existing one?
faq.answer: Create a new ticket for new work items. Update an existing ticket when expanding vague issues or adding new requirements.
